finding: Age of children: usually 6-9 months, up to 2 years

differentials.name: poor weight gain, vomiting, aspiration, choking
differentials.raw: poor weight gain, vomiting, aspiration, choking

differentials.name: asthmatic episodes, stridor, apnea Detection:   upper GI examination with barium, distal esophageal sphincter pressure measurements, 24-hr pH probe measurement in distal esophagus (gold standard), radionuclide examination Preparation:   4-hour / overnight fasting; abdominal sphygmomanometer (for adults) Dose:     0.5-1.0 mCi 99mTc-sulfur colloid in 300 mL of acidified orange juice (150 mL juice + 150 mL 0.1 N hydrochloric acid) followed by "cold" acidified orange juice Imaging:   at 30-60-second intervals for 30-60 min images taken in supine position from anterior; sphygmo-manometer inflated at 20, 40, 60, 80, 100 mmHg
differentials.raw: asthmatic episodes, stridor, apnea Detection:   upper GI examination with barium, distal esophageal sphincter pressure measurements, 24-hr pH probe measurement in distal esophagus (gold standard), radionuclide examination Preparation:   4-hour / overnight fasting; abdominal sphygmomanometer (for adults) Dose:     0.5-1.0 mCi 99mTc-sulfur colloid in 300 mL of acidified orange juice (150 mL juice + 150 mL 0.1 N hydrochloric acid) followed by "cold" acidified orange juice Imaging:   at 30-60-second intervals for 30-60 min images taken in supine position from anterior; sphygmo-manometer inflated at 20, 40, 60, 80, 100 mmHg

differentials.name: Self-limiting process with spontaneous resolution by end of infancy
differentials.raw: Self-limiting process with spontaneous resolution by end of infancy (in majority of patients)

differentials.name: Persistent symptoms until age 4
differentials.raw: Persistent symptoms until age 4 (⅓ of patients)

differentials.name: Cause of recurrent respiratory infections, asthma, failure to thrive, esophagitis, esophageal stricture, chronic blood loss, sudden infant death syndrome
differentials.raw: Cause of recurrent respiratory infections, asthma, failure to thrive, esophagitis, esophageal stricture, chronic blood loss, sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS)

context: Interpretation: Reflux (in %) =  [counts esophageal - counts background] / counts gastric) • 100 √  up to 3% magnitude reflux is normal √  evidence of pulmonary aspiration (valuable in pediatric age group) Cx:     reflux esophagitis secondary to (a)  delayed clearance time of esophageal acid load: tertiary / repetitive esophageal contractions, supine position of refluxor, aspiration of saliva, stimulation of salivary flow, stretched phrenoesophageal membrane in hiatal hernia (b)  delayed gastric emptying: increased intragastric pressure (gastric outlet obstruction), viral gastropathy, diabetes
